articles.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico star Mora's high school graduation, France-Morocco kicks off quarterfinals

articles.headline: Morocco press conference chaos leaves Brahim Diaz forgetting the question

articles.headline: Lamine Yamal: 'Incredible' to see Lionel Messi still starring at World Cup
articles.description: Spain winger Lamine Yamal has said he is thrilled to see Lionel Messi defy time and continue to have a starring role at this World Cup.

images.name: Lamine Yamal [1296x729]
images.credit: Photo by Grzegorz Wajda/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Images

images.name: Why Spain's substitutes were so important vs. Portugal
images.caption: Alex Kirkland reacts to Spain's late 1-0 win over Portugal to advance to the quarterfinals.

articles.headline: Keir Starmer hints at bank holiday if England win World Cup
articles.description: An England World Cup win could mean an extra bank holiday, Sir Keir Starmer has hinted ahead of a quarterfinal clash with Norway.

images.name: Keir Starmer [1296x729]
images.caption: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store greet the media at the Nato summit
images.alt: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store greet the media at the Nato summit

images.name: Harry Kane Jude Bellingham [1296x729]
images.caption: Harry Kane (left) and Jude Bellingham (right) celebrate Kane's goal for England on Sunday.

images.name: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'
images.caption: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'

articles.headline: Morocco star Saibari to miss World Cup quarterfinal vs. France
articles.description: Morocco will be without star forward Ismael Saibari for their World Cup quarterfinal with France on Thursday.

images.name: Ismael Saibari [1296x729]
images.caption: Ismael Saibari suffered an injury in Morocco's win over Canada.
images.alt: Ismael Saibari #11 (C) of Morocco reacts after sustaining an injury during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 match between Canada and Morocco at Houston Stadium on July 04, 2026 in Houston, Texas.

article.headline: Lionel Messi equals World Cup scoring record with hat trick against Algeria
article.description: Lionel Messi equalled Miroslav Klose's record of 16 World Cup goals with a hat-trick as Argentina began their title defence with a 3-0 win over Algeria in Kansas City.
article.linkText: Messi nets record hat trick in 3-0 win over Algeria

images.name: Argentina fans celebrate Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria
images.caption: Argentina fans celebrate Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria

video.headline: Argentina fans in Buenos Aires react to Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria
video.shortHeadline: Argentina fans celebrate Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria
video.caption: Argentina fans celebrate Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria
video.title: Argentina fans celebrate Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria
video.description: Argentina fans celebrate Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria
video.shortDescription: Argentina fans in Buenos Aires react to Messi's hat trick goal vs. Algeria

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (Argentina fans in Buenos Aires react to Messi’s hat trick goal vs. Algeria) 2026/06/16 GLOBAL FIFA WORLD CUP

article.story:
Lionel Messi equalled Miroslav Klose's record of 16 World Cup goals with a hat-trick as Argentina began their title defence with a 3-0 win over Algeria in Kansas City.

Argentina had started their ultimately successful 2022 campaign with defeat to Saudi Arabia, but avoided any such upset on this occasion thanks to Messi's first World Cup treble.

Messi's second took him level with Brazil great Ronaldo on 15 goals across his six World Cup appearances.

He was denied in a one-on-one by Algeria goalkeeper Luca Zidane and had a penalty appeal turned down before joining Germany's Klose in a share of the record.

The 38-year-old will have chances to move into the outright lead in Argentina's remaining Group J games against Austria and Jordan next week - assuming France's Kylian Mbappé, who is on 14 after a brace against Senegal, does not run riot against Iraq on Monday.

Messi thought he had opened the scoring after only five minutes, but was denied by a tight offside call after a composed finish into the bottom corner.

Four minutes later it was Algeria's turn to be denied - this time after VAR overruled the original decision when Ibrahim Maza played in Farès Chaïbi and he put a low effort past Emi Martinez.

It proved to be the only attempt on target a toothless Algeria side - which surprisingly saw Riyad Mahrez dropped to the bench - could muster all evening.

Messi opened the scoring after 17 minutes as he linked up with Inter Miami team-mate Rodrigo de Paul and fired a 20-yard effort into the roof of the net.

Chaibi wasted an excellent chance to equalise and, at the other end, Thiago Almada fired over before the break.

Lautaro Martínez shot straight at Algeria goalkeeper Zidane - the son of France World Cup winner Zinedine - who also saved well from Alexis Mac Allister on the hour mark, only for Messi to react quickest and turn in the rebound.

Mac Allister's defence-splitting pass sent Messi clear after 66 minutes, but Zidane was equal to his effort and referee Szymon Marciniak waved away the Inter Miami forward's penalty claims after Manchester City defender Rayan Aït-Nouri sent him tumbling in the box.

Messi's moment did finally arrive in the 76th minute as he collected Nicolas Gonzalez's pass and fired a low effort into the bottom corner.

Messi made way for Nico Paz a short time afterwards to a standing ovation from the Arrowhead Stadium crowd as Argentina comfortably saw out an opening win.

stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.name: duelsWon

stats.name: groundDuels

stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)
